---
title: "Ibtesam Ifaz"
subtitle: "Data Analyst · Melbourne, Australia"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero">
  <p class="lead">
    I turn messy, real-world datasets into clear visual stories.
    My work spans urban analytics, political data, and global policy —
    all built in R.
  </p>
</div>
```

## Projects

```{=html}
<div class="card-grid">

  <div class="project-card">
    <div class="card-tag">Urban Analytics</div>
    <h3><a href="cafes_vs_pubs.qmd">Cafés vs Pubs in Melbourne</a></h3>
    <p>
      Mapping how Melbourne's café and pub culture is distributed across
      neighbourhoods, and how that balance has shifted over two decades —
      linked to income and age demographics.
    </p>
    <div class="card-tools">ggplot2 · sf · viridis · ABS Census</div>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <div class="card-tag">Political Data</div>
    <h3><a href="election_analytics.qmd">National Election Analytics</a></h3>
    <p>
      Analysing Australian federal election results from 2019 and 2022,
      with a focus on independent candidates — which seats were won, lost,
      or flipped, and the swing patterns behind them.
    </p>
    <div class="card-tools">ggplot2 · AEC data · patchwork · ggrepel</div>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <div class="card-tag">Global Policy</div>
    <h3><a href="military_spending_report.qmd">Military Spending Report</a></h3>
    <p>
      Tracking military expenditure trends across Australia, China, India,
      and Russia from 2012–2023, and exploring the relationship between
      spending, GDP share, and population.
    </p>
    <div class="card-tools">ggplot2 · tidyverse · SIPRI data</div>
  </div>

</div>
```

## About

I'm a data analytics student at Monash University with a focus on
visualisation and applied statistics. I work primarily in R, using
tools like `ggplot2`, `sf`, and Quarto to communicate findings clearly.

Feel free to reach out via [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ibtesam-ifaz-a16a48183/)
or explore my code on [GitHub](https://github.com/ibtesamifaz).
